questions.question: What is the primary purpose of using a kernel size in a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN)?
options: A) To increase the number of layers in the network
B) To decrease the number of parameters in the network
C) To increase the spatial resolution of the input data
D) To decrease the spatial resolution of the input data
questions.explanation: The kernel size determines the spatial scope of the convolution operation, allowing the network to extract features from larger regions of the input data.

questions.question: What is the main difference between a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) and a Feedforward Neural Network (FNN)?
options: A) CNN uses a different activation function
B) CNN uses a different type of pooling layer
C) CNN uses convolutional and pooling layers to extract features from input data
D) CNN uses recurrent connections to model sequential data
questions.explanation: CNNs are designed to extract features from spatial hierarchies, whereas FNNs are designed to model sequential data.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the 'padding' parameter in a CNN?
options: A) To increase the size of the input data
B) To decrease the number of parameters in the network
C) To maintain the spatial resolution of the input data
D) To increase the number of layers in the network
questions.explanation: Padding is used to maintain the spatial resolution of the input data during convolutional layers, preventing the loss of features at the border of the input data.

questions.question: What is the main advantage of using batch normalization in a CNN?
options: A) To reduce the number of parameters in the network
B) To increase the speed of training
C) To stabilize the training process and improve the quality of feature extraction
D) To decrease the spatial resolution of the input data
questions.explanation: Batch normalization helps to stabilize the training process and improve the quality of feature extraction by normalizing the inputs to each layer.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the 'dropout' layer in a CNN?
options: A) To prevent overfitting
B) To increase the speed of training
C) To decrease the number of parameters in the network
D) To increase the spatial resolution of the input data
questions.explanation: Dropout is a regularization technique used to prevent overfitting by randomly dropping out units during training, forcing the network to learn multiple representations of the input data.
